skills.name: Vendor Invoice Completeness – AP
skills.shortDescription: Three-way match, PO completeness, and split-payment detection for AP invoices above materiality.

step1.auditObjective: Test that all vendor invoices greater than $10,000 have an approved purchase order and a matching goods receipt on file. Identify any payments made without dual-authorisation controls and detect potential split-payment schemes.
step1.population: All AP invoices posted to ERP during the audit period

tests.name: Invoices Without Approved PO
tests.objective: Identify all invoices above materiality threshold processed without a valid PO reference.

tests.name: Three-Way Match Variance
tests.objective: Identify invoices where the invoiced amount differs from the PO amount beyond tolerance.

tests.name: Split Payment Detection
tests.objective: Detect potential invoice-splitting where same vendor submits invoices below threshold that cumulatively exceed it.

tests.name: High-Value Invoice Sample Review
tests.objective: Risk-based sample of highest-value invoices to verify documentation completeness.

recommendedActions.T01: Management should investigate each exception, obtain retroactive PO documentation where available, and implement a system-level preventive control to block payments without a valid PO reference above the materiality threshold.
recommendedActions.T02: Management should review each variance, document the business rationale, and ensure three-way match reconciliation is evidenced for all invoices above materiality prior to payment release.
recommendedActions.T03: Management must investigate each split-payment cluster with urgency. Engage the fraud investigation team if the pattern cannot be explained by a legitimate blanket order. Place vendor under enhanced monitoring.
recommendedActions.T04: Management should ensure all required documentation is obtained and implement a documentation checklist at the point of invoice approval.

versions.author: jane.smith@acme.com
versions.changes: Threshold $5k→$10k. T03 window 7→3 days. Exception count −70%.

skills.name: AP Duplicate Payment Detection
skills.shortDescription: Identifies duplicate payments using vendor + amount + date proximity. Flags split invoices below approval thresholds.

step1.auditObjective: Detect duplicate payments to the same vendor for the same or similar amount within a rolling window, indicating potential fraud or processing error.
step1.population: All AP payments processed in the period

tests.name: Exact Duplicate Invoices
tests.objective: Find invoices with identical vendor + amount + date — exact duplicates.

tests.name: Near-Duplicate Payments (Same Vendor, ±5 days)
tests.objective: Detect same-vendor payments of the same amount within a 5-day window.

recommendedActions.T01: Investigate each duplicate pair immediately. Recover overpayments where duplicate payments were made. Review AP system controls to prevent future duplicate processing.
recommendedActions.T02: Review near-duplicate clusters and determine if legitimate repeat deliveries or error payments. Implement system-level duplicate detection at time of invoice entry.

skills.name: Days Delay
skills.shortDescription: Delays between the document date and the posting date, indicating potential process inefficiencies, control lapses, or intentional backdating.

step1.auditObjective:
This audit is designed to detect and quantify delays in posting accounting documents by comparing the document date with the posting date, ensuring compliance with internal timelines and accounting policies.

It helps identify:

Late postings beyond acceptable thresholds
Backdated entries used to manipulate reporting periods
Operational bottlenecks in finance workflows

tests.name: High Delay Transactions Sampling  Objective
tests.objective: To select a focused sample of transactions with the highest posting delays for detailed audit review, ensuring that significant timing deviations are investigated.

recommendedActions.High: Immediate escalation to CFO and Audit Committee; initiate root-cause investigation.
recommendedActions.Medium: Management to provide written response within 30 days; internal audit to validate remediation.
recommendedActions.Low: Process owner to acknowledge and document corrective action in next cycle.

tests.name: High-Value Sample Review
tests.objective: Select a risk-based sample of highest-value transactions for substantive documentation testing.

recommendedActions.High: Immediate escalation to senior management; root-cause investigation within 5 business days.
recommendedActions.Medium: Management response within 30 days; audit to validate remediation in next cycle.
recommendedActions.Low: Process owner to document corrective action by next audit cycle.

findings.recommendation: Management should review each variance, document the business rationale, and ensure three-way match reconciliation is evidenced for all invoices above materiality prior to payment release.
findings.condition: 416 exceptions (41.60% of 1000 in-scope records) identified for "Three-Way Match Variance".

findings.recommendation: Management must investigate each split-payment cluster with urgency. Engage the fraud investigation team if the pattern cannot be explained by a legitimate blanket order. Place vendor under enhanced monitoring.
findings.condition: 6 exceptions (0.60% of 1000 in-scope records) identified for "Split Payment Detection".

findings.recommendation: Management should ensure all required documentation is obtained and implement a documentation checklist at the point of invoice approval.
findings.condition: 2 of 25 sampled items (8.0%) failed documentation review for "High-Value Invoice Sample Review".
